high_concern_flags: prompt_verification: HFLL 100-employee threshold verified against Hawaii DLIR Wage Standards Division + Nolo + The Hartford + paywizard.org — all four agree (Haw. Rev. Stat. ch. 398)
prompt_verification: HFLL 4-week unpaid entitlement and 6-month-consecutive-service eligibility verified; no minimum hours rule (gentler than FMLA) verified

prompt_verification: HFLL retain-15-days-of-sick-leave rule verified against DLIR Wage Standards Division — Hawaii-specific protection that prevents employer forcing full sick balance to be spent down during HFLL
prompt_verification: TDI 58% wage replacement, $871 weekly cap, 26-week max duration, 7-day waiting period all verified against Hawaii DLIR DCD About TDI page + Prudential 2026 update + The Hartford
prompt_verification: no state PFL for bonding verified — TDI covers ONLY medically certified disability, not parental bonding (consistent across DLIR DCD, MetLife, Guardian, Prudential sources)
prompt_verification: no state PSL verified — Hawaii is one of ~35 states with no state-level paid sick leave statute

factual_uncertainty (low): PHC during-leave employer share continuation rule cited from Hawaii Prepaid Health Care Act ch. 393 implementation guidance — verify against latest DLIR DCD bulletin if a client client engages on PHC-during-leave specifically
scheduled_data_update: 2026 TDI weekly cap ($871) and PHC monthly earnings trigger ($1,387) both reset each January — calendar reminder for January 2027

editorial_v2_applied: capsule pattern (stake-size-ack-promise), zero statutes in body prose (all in source columns), 3 named scenarios (Kai/Leilani/Makoa), 1 metaphor avoided, named scenarios carry dollar figures, single end-of-page disclaimer

review_queue_reasons: First Hawaii leave page in sprint — needs eyeball verification that HFLL 100-employee framing reads cleanly against the more-typical 50/5-employee state thresholds in California/Colorado/NJ siblings

TDI as wage-replacement-only (not job-protection) is a subtle distinction that risks confusing buyers who arrive expecting 'Hawaii has PFL like California' — verify the no-state-PFL framing is firm without sounding like a Hawaii deficiency
PHC-during-leave employer-share continuation rule + employee-share collection mechanic during unpaid leave is a buyer-pain section that should match Teamed Client Operations field experience
